channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Toronto patient shows potential HIV cure following stem cell transplant
item.description: A Toronto man may become the first Canadian to be considered cured of HIV after undergoing a stem cell transplant in 2021 to treat life-threatening cancer. The patient, who is 62 and requested anonymi...

item.title: Trump says no prior intelligence indicated threats before White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description: President Donald Trump stated that neither he nor his team had received any prior warning about potential threats before a shooting incident occurred at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner (WHCD). ...

item.title: Okamoto leads Blue Jays past Guardians with second consecutive home run in 5-3 win
item.description: Kazuma Okamoto continued his strong offensive run by hitting a home run for the second consecutive game, helping the Toronto Blue Jays secure a 5-3 victory over the Cleveland Guardians. Okamoto delive...

item.title: Trump attends White House press dinner amid unsettling incident
item.description: For the first time in over a decade, Donald Trump attended the annual black-tie dinner honouring journalists who cover the White House. The event, a tradition that allows presidents to lightly roast j...

item.title: Exploring Northern Cyprus: Life in a Partially Recognized State
item.description: This article chronicles a traveler's journey through Northern Cyprus, a territory recognized only by Turkey, highlighting its unique geopolitical and cultural context. The author describes crossing th...

item.title: Exploring the Role of Mopane Worm and Termite Trade in Reducing Poverty in Rural South Africa
item.description: In South Africa’s Limpopo province, known for its extreme poverty, local communities are turning to the commercialisation of mopane worms and termite alates to alleviate poverty. Studies reveal that t...

item.title: Dangote plans large-scale refinery in East Africa to cut fuel imports
item.description: Nigerian business mogul Aliko Dangote dey plan expand him oil refining business go East Africa, with plans to build one big refinery for either Tanzania or Kenya. Dis new refinery go follow di model o...

item.title: Examining Femicide in South Africa: Causes, Consequences and Prevention
item.description: Femicide in South Africa represents a deeply rooted societal problem, where women are intentionally killed because of their gender. Recent cases, like that of Vikash and Unitha Rajwanta in Durban Nort...

item.title: Witness recounts shooting incident at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: A server at the Washington Hilton has shared a chilling account of the moments leading up to a shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner. As she and a colleague pushed a service cart into an ...

item.title: ODM Leaders Visit Raila Odinga's Grave Before Kisumu Rally
item.description: The Linda Mwananchi faction of ODM, led by Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna, Siaya Governor James Orengo, and Embakasi East MP Babu Owino, visited the grave of former party leader Raila Odinga in Bondo, S...

item.title: The Rise of Sanitised Biopics and Their Dominance in Hollywood
item.description: In recent years, Hollywood has embraced the rise of sanitised biopics that prioritise fan service over truthfulness. Films like 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and the upcoming 'Michael' have shown that audiences...

item.title: How Social Media Supports the Mental Health of Filipino Youth
item.description: In the Philippines, social media has evolved from a simple communication tool into a vital support system for young people navigating mental health challenges. Research conducted by Harvard Medical Sc...

item.title: Trump and officials safe after attempted shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: On Saturday night at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner held at the Washington Hilton, US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were safely evacuated by Secret Service ag...

item.title: Nigeria quietly cancels $32.8m Meta fine after settlement with weaker data protection terms
item.description: Nigeria government later cancelled the $32.8 million fine wey dem place on Meta (owner of Facebook and Instagram) over data privacy violations after reaching settlement in October 2025. The fine come ...

item.title: WhatsApp Messages Detail Alleged Kidnapping Plot of Pretoria Businessman
item.description: A chilling investigation has revealed how two henchmen of alleged criminal boss, Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala, reportedly plotted the kidnapping of Pretoria businessman Jerry Boshega in November 2024. Whats...

item.title: Renowned Photographer Raghu Rai Passes Away at 83
item.description: Raghu Rai, one of India’s most celebrated photographers, passed away at the age of 83 in a private hospital in Delhi on Sunday. According to his son, Nitin Rai, he had been fighting cancer for the pas...

item.title: Exploring Iran's Nuclear Program: Military Ambitions or Civilian Energy Focus?
item.description: Dr. Mehran Mostafavi, a nuclear expert and professor at Université Paris-Saclay, offers insights into the long-standing debate about Iran's nuclear program. He clarifies that while Iran's nuclear ambi...

item.title: Punjab Signs Agreement to Export Livestock and Boost Meat Industry
item.description: LAHORE: Punjab government has taken a major step towards boosting its livestock and meat sector by signing a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with seven institutions, including a Chinese company, aim...

item.title: Former Presidents Challenge Chair of Apartheid-Era Crimes Inquiry
item.description: Former South African presidents Thabo Mbeki and Jacob Zuma have jointly challenged the role of Sisi Khampepe as chair of the judicial commission investigating potential political interference in the p...

item.title: Edwin Sodi faces possible property auction over unpaid estate levies
item.description: Controversial businessman Edwin Sodi is facing fresh financial pressure after falling nearly two years behind on levy payments for a luxury apartment in Dainfern, Johannesburg. The body corporate of t...
